task: Write a Python function count_distinct_over(nums, k) returning how many distinct values in the list are strictly greater than k, each value counted once no matter how many times it repeats. An empty list returns 0. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The solution meets all criteria, including correctly handling an empty list as an edge case.
trace:
1. To avoid duplicate counting of elements and to make the function more efficient, I converted the input list into a set.
2. Then, I used a generator expression to iterate through the set and count only the numbers greater than `k`. This approach guarantees that each number will be counted once no matter how many times it repeats in the original list.
